channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The Year of the Snake is underway with Lunar New Year festivities in Asia and around the world
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Firecrackers popped, drummers and dancers paraded and incense was offered Wednesday at temples around Asia and farther afield as millions around the world celebrated the Lunar New Year.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Top-tier college student, former high school football star killed in freak accident
item.description: Authorities confirmed that a Clemson University student died after he was crushed and trapped by a fallen tree in a freak accident over the weekend in South Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump makes moves to expand power, sparking chaos, possible constitutional crisis
item.description: Just a little over a week into his second term, President Donald Trump is taking steps to maximize his power, sparking chaos and what critics contend is a constitutional crisis as he challenges the separation of powers that have defined American government for more than 200 years.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: WATCH: Miles-long cluster of dolphins captured on drone video
item.description: A miles-long cluster of dolphins has been filmed leaping and gliding across Carmel Bay off California, forming an unusual “super pod” of more than 1,500 of the marine creatures.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Obama-era prosecutor's probe into blue state police racial bias claims called 'untenable' for troopers
item.description: New Jersey Attorney General Matthew Platkin has ordered a special counsel probe into state police after a slowdown in traffic stops reportedly led to more crashes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Deeply disturbing statistics show Texas children under the age of 11 left state for abortion in 2023
item.description: Newly released statistics from 2023 show that minors in Texas are traveling out of the state to have abortions and the numbers are rising.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Flawed emergency alert systems lagged when residents needed them most during Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — When disaster strikes, government emergency alert systems offer a simple promise: Residents will get information about nearby dangers and instructions to help them stay safe.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, CLAUDIA LAUER, AMY TAXIN and REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump plans to sign the Laken Riley Act into law as his administration's first piece of legislation
item.description: President Donald Trump plans to sign the Laken Riley Act into law as his administration's first piece of legislation.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A $4B settlement for Hawaii wildfire victims is in legal limbo as an unusual trial starts
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — When Hawaii Gov. Josh Green announced a $4 billion settlement about a year after the deadliest U.S. wildfire in a century devastated Lahaina in 2023, he touted the speed of the deal to “avoid protracted and painful lawsuits.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge set to sentence former Sen. Bob Menendez, who was convicted of taking bribes of cash and gold
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former U.S. Sen. Bob Menendez faces the possibility of a long prison term when he is sentenced Wednesday for selling his once-considerable clout in Washington for gold bars, a luxury car and hundreds of thousands of dollars in cash bribes.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: F-35 fighter jet crashes at Eielson Air Force Base in Alaska, pilot taken to hospital
item.description: An F-35 fighter jet crashed Tuesday after an in-flight malfunction at Eielson Air Force Base in Alaska, sending the pilot to the hospital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nevada will provide U.S. Capitol Christmas Tree for the first time
item.description: Nevada will be front and center this year as it provides the U.S. Capitol Christmas Tree for the first time ever.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Scottie Scheffler details his hand injury from making ravioli and is ready to get back to golf
item.description: Scottie Scheffler felt frustrated sitting at home for two tournaments he normally plays while recovering from glass puncturing the the palm of his right hand while making ravioli. A big part of him was thankful the injury wasn't worse.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump order aims to end federal support for gender transitions for those under 19
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order aimed at cutting federal support for gender transitions for people under age 19, his latest move to roll back protections for transgender people across the country.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, GEOFF MULVIHILL and HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Weeks after fire ravaged LA's Pacific Palisades, residents return to dig for mementos
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Scores of Los Angeles residents returned Tuesday to wildfire-ravaged Pacific Palisades after officials began letting people drive back into the scorched community, the first opportunity most have had to carefully scour the sites of their former homes.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER and EUGENE GARCIA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump fires two Democratic commissioners of agency that enforces civil rights laws in the workplace
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — President Donald Trump fired two of the three Democratic commissioners of the federal agency that enforces civil rights law in the workplace, an unprecedented move aimed at implementing his crackdown on certain diversity and gender rights policies.
item.dc:creator: ALEXANDRA OLSON and CLAIRE SAVAGE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Disturbing messages seen in empty lot in downtown Los Angeles are not nefarious: LAPD
item.description: A patch of undeveloped land in downtown Los Angeles is going viral on social media for some distressing messages seen from above, but police have confirmed that they found no illicit activity in the area.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, WGN 9

item.title: Man busted with 15 pounds of meth, 13 pounds of cocaine in Florida: Deputies
item.description: A search warrant on his Volusia County home revealed cash and packaging consistent with narcotics trafficking.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, NewsNation

item.title: US education agency investigating Denver schools over all-gender bathroom
item.description: DENVER (AP) — The U.S. Education Department said Tuesday it was investigating Denver Public Schools for alleged discrimination after the district converted a girl’s restroom on the second floor of a high school into an all-gender restroom while leaving another bathroom exclusive to boys.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New Homeland Security secretary joins federal agents to tout immigrant arrests in NYC
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem joined federal agents Tuesday in New York City to announce the arrests of immigrants wanted on criminal charges, including a man accused of kidnapping who was seen on a viral video entering a Colorado apartment with reputed gang members.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Largest documented tuberculosis outbreak in US history hits Kansas, officials say
item.description: The Kansas Department of Health and Environment (KDHE) says Kansas is facing the largest documented tuberculosis outbreak in American history.
item.dc:creator: Colter Robinson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ousted IG: Trump firings a 'a potentially existential threat' to government oversight
item.description: One of 17 government watchdogs ousted by President Trump said that the move was a “a potentially existential threat” to a crucial government oversight tool.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump administration freeze on federal grants and loans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge has temporarily blocked a Trump administration freeze on federal grants and loans that could total trillions of dollars. U.S. District Judge Loren L. AliKhan blocked the action Tuesday afternoon, minutes before it was set to go into effect.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Capitol Police arrest man with Molotov cocktails allegedly targeting GOP politicians
item.description: Officers said they found a folding knife “as well as two destructive devices from the inside pockets of ENGLISH’s jacket” when they searched Ryan English.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Watch a miles-long cluster of dolphins captured on drone video
item.description: A miles-long cluster of dolphins has been filmed leaping and gliding across Carmel Bay off the central coast of California, forming an unusual “super pod” of more than 1,500 of the marine creatures.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 Tampa Bay area restaurants make list of most romantic in the US, according to OpenTable
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Valentine's Day is just around the corner and finding the perfect restaurant to take the one you love can always be challenging.
item.dc:creator: Nick Jachim, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supplemental Security Income: Why February's checks are coming early
item.description: Supplemental Security Income (SSI) beneficiaries include those with disabilities and older adults with little to no income.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Workers save kidnapped girl at Dothan hotel, police say
item.description: A Florida man is behind bars after police say he sexually assaulted and kidnapped a girl at an Alabama hotel.
item.dc:creator: Richard Everett, NewsNation

item.title: Pence group uses Trump's criticism against RFK Jr. in new ad
item.description: An advocacy group founded by former Vice President Mike Pence is escalating its fight against Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Why February's Supplemental Security Income checks are coming early
item.description: If you receive Supplemental Security Income from the Social Security Administration, you know the last two payments have been a bit wonky.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Coca-Cola launches new Orange Cream flavor
item.description: A year after the launch of the short-lived Coca-Cola Spiced, Coke is adding another new flavor to its lineup.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Indiana man pardoned by Trump is fatally shot during a traffic stop
item.description: RENSSELAER, Ind. (AP) — An Indiana man was fatally shot by a deputy during a traffic stop just days after he was pardoned by President Donald Trump for a misdemeanor related to the 2021 U.S. Capitol riot.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Proposal would require parents to provide immigration status of students
item.description: “OKCPS does not, nor do we have plans to, collect the immigration status of our students or their families,” she said.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 3.8 magnitude earthquake in Maine felt in Massachusetts, Rhode Island
item.description: A Ware, Massachusetts, resident said she was home and her whole house was shaking for about 10 seconds from what she at first believed was an 18-wheeler coming down Route 9.
item.dc:creator: Emma McCorkindale, WGN 9
